title: Strobilanthes ridleyi
intro: Strobilanthes ridleyi, also known as the Blue Pencilled Plant or Blue Strobilanthes, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, boasting various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its potential in treating conditions like fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Strobilanthes ridleyi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Strobilanthes ridleyi is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and dried extracts, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various ailments.

This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Acanthoideae, which is where Strobilanthes ridleyi is placed. Strobilanthes ridleyi is a species within the genus Strobilanthes, which is a group of plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia.
